title: Family Preservation Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Family Preservation Programs 58 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Substance Use Disorder Issues 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program focuses on early childhood development through case management home visits that include parent education and skill building, developmental and health screenings, kindergarten readiness and transition, and navigating community resources. Services parenting classes, support groups, case management, advocacy with Child Welfare System, and resource navigation. 310 NE Failing Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Must be referred by DHS Child Welfare Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 285-0493 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMETTE FAMILY INC WILLAMETTE FAMILY SUNRISE HOUSE & FAMILIES IN RECOVERY FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers two family programs for those in residential/outpatient substance addiction treatment: -Family Reunion Program for DHS-referred families: Program provides services to assist with preventing children from entering the foster care system and/or being reunited with their families after having been in foster care. Services include family advocacy, visitation, home visits, co-residency for children through age 5 whose mothers are in residential treatment, parent education, and mentoring. -Family Services: During residential treatment, clients have access to family advocates who assist families with parenting-related needs. Eligibility: Families in which one or both parents are in substance abuse and/or mental health treatment through Willamette Family. Priority to families where children are at risk of removal from family to be placed into foster care, or who are in foster care and are being reunited with their parents.
